ascent.comment: Pose B: x = grade/course timeline (band-relaxed, as pose A x), y = longest-prerequisite-chain depth * yStep (graph invariant: every prereq edge points upward), z = relaxed cross-section z * zScale.

blueprint.comment: Pose C: flat 2D circuit board echoing Achieve the Core's original coherence map, given AIR and LIFT (round 11). 13 columns left to right (K, 1..8, then Appendix A courses A1/G/A2/ADV; the 23 dual A1&A2 standards sit in A1 only). x = column center (columnWidth apart, row centered on 0). Within a column, standards group into DOMAIN BLOCKS in canonical code order (the original map's own grouping, separated by blockGapFactor*rowGap — opened up so each block reads as a separate paper card with real gaps between them), and a barycenter heuristic reorders rows WITHIN each block only: every prerequisite neighbor pulls with weight 1/column-distance (same-column neighbors at 0.5), barycenterSweeps alternating sweeps. Rows re-spaced uniformly at rowGap = min(rowGapMax, targetColumnHeight / tallestColumn), each column centered on y=0. Isolated (edgeless) nodes sit in a side gutter (+18) stacked from the column top. THE LIFT (like an architecture diagram come to life / pop-up cards): each DOMAIN BLOCK rides its own z-plane — block k in canonical order at z = 8 + 7k, capped at 40 — and the isolated gutter is one quiet plane at z = 4. Front-on (x/y) the schematic is UNCHANGED; the lift only reads on a side orbit as layered paper. Same-column edges all bow LEFT (fixed convention; depth encodes hop span up to sameColumnBow); every control point keeps its front-on bow x/y and sets z to the midpoint of its endpoints' z. Grade/course etch labels sit on one shared baseline labelDrop below the deepest column, front-on.

transit.comment: Pose D: octolinear metro map, derived from the transfer graph (see scripts/formation-previews.mjs transit(), the approved derivation). Families contract to UNITS; per strand the MAIN LINE is the maximum-reach weighted path (DP+traceback), everything else chains as a branch onto its primary within-strand prerequisite (trunkless standards join the grade-nearest station), and heavy cross-strand transfers (>= 3 cross-strand prerequisites) are multi-line interchanges the guest line routes through. x = the SAME 13 blueprint columns (columnWidth apart, centered on 0) so the poses rhyme, with a per-column depth band up to bandHalfMax; y solved by 12-pass barycenter crossing-minimisation (ties by code), scaled to pos3's y span (rowHeight capped at rowHeightMax). THE THIRD DIMENSION: each LINE runs at its own z-level (number +90 / algebra +30 / geometry -30 / data -90 — decks pushed far apart so a side orbit reads the interwebs), a layered city elevated to deep; a station's z is its OWN strand's level and interchanges sit at their host level, the guest line reaching them via banked edge ramps (c4). Family children re-expand in a tight row next to the parent station (childSpacing apart) at the parent's z. Deterministic: derived from the DAG, no Math.random / Date.
